reviews.review_msg: I enjoyed this class. Dr. Eddie gave us many opportunities to discuss things in class and ask him questions. Gives hints in class about what you should know for the exam and very helpful during office hours. OK load for an MBB class.

Eddie is an involved and genuine prof and he really cares about the success of the class. Lectures were very engaging and exams were fair. I would definitely take more courses with him. As a side note, he is also quite funny
Terrible lectures that involve him just reading off slides posted online but he has 5% iclicker only if you attend more than 80% of lectures

Basically, you can know the lecture slides inside out and still do bad because he expects you to apply your knowledge in really weird ways. His thick accent makes it difficult to understand him and he talks really fast. Tutorials were very irrelevant and a waste of time, but they were mandatory

I enjoyed this class. Dr. Eddie gave us many opportunities to discuss things in class and ask him questions. Gives hints in class about what you should know for the exam and very helpful during office hours. OK load for an MBB class.

Eddie is an involved and genuine prof and he really cares about the success of the class. Lectures were very engaging and exams were fair. I would definitely take more courses with him. As a side note, he is also quite funny
Terrible lectures that involve him just reading off slides posted online but he has 5% iclicker only if you attend more than 80% of lectures

Basically, you can know the lecture slides inside out and still do bad because he expects you to apply your knowledge in really weird ways. His thick accent makes it difficult to understand him and he talks really fast. Tutorials were very irrelevant and a waste of time, but they were mandatory

Tests were fair and expectation was clearly communicated. Lecture slides are good, contain enough info, you don't need to furiously take notes during lectures. Speaks slowly, so I watched the recording at 1.4x speed. Really advocated for us to the IT dept. to have the lecture recordings available. Good at explaining things esp. during office hours.

Dr.Pio is by far one of the most unbearable teachers I've ever had. His accent makes it far too difficult to understand during lecture and his monotone voice doesn't help that cause. Tests are solely based off lecture content so there's no point in showing up. Just study his slides and you'll do fine. Definitely prepare to teach yourself

yes he has a heavy French accent but his tests are identical to what you learn in the lecture slides/practice tests/tutorial worksheets. We did have to purchase a textbook but I did not read it and got close to 100% on all my tests just by studying the slides. 3 quizzes a week online, open book, like 3-7 questions. 
Tests are all non cumulative :D

Professor Pio was a nice guy, but clearly not interested in teaching. He seemed bored by the material, read in a monotone voice and basically read straight from the slides. The best way I can describe his extremely heavy accent is that it felt like I had the subtitles in front of me and yet still couldn't decipher what he was trying to say.

Exams were very fair. Final exam was cumulative but manageable as he gave many hints during review sessions. He was very accessible for extra help. He will explain concepts over and over again until he is convinced you actually understand.

Virtually impossible to understand and not at all any more helpful during his office hours. Exams are reasonable other than the 2 questions he'll teach incorrectly or take from lectures that aren't supposed to be included. One of the worst professors I've ever had.

Good luck trying to understand him. Attend lectures for the iclickers and self-teach the rest. Follow the SLOs on the lecture slides and look at diagrams in the textbook to do well on the exams. His practice exams are very similar to the midterms and finals. Overall not a very tough prof if you can learn the material on your own.
The midterm & final are from the notes Very fair. Very knowledgeable & the accent youll get used to I. A few days

Don't know why everyone hates on him so much, he isn't the most interesting prof and he has an accent but he doesn't deserve all the flack that he gets. He's a nice guy and exams are relatively easy, plus they're marked fair. He tries many times to be engaging, it's just that most people in this course(myself included) don't care much about MBB..
He is the worst port I have ever seen.

He has a thick French accent but you eventually get used to it. Not as bad as people say, though not great either. Lectures are boring but the material he covers in class is usually the stuff that will show up on quizzes or midterms.

He's not THAT bad of a professor as people say. The accent - you'll get used to after a week. His lectures are boring but the midterm and final were straight from his notes. His quizzes are not hard either, you just have to read the textbook and teach yourself what material is relevant from it by looking at his learning outcomes.

exams and quizzes ridiculously hard. he does not test main concepts. make sure you know random, small details because that is what is going to be on the exams. you do not need to show up to lectures, you will not be able to understand him. read the textbook. only way you will learn
Terrible prof, can't understand him, only reason to go to lecture is for participation mark. He has a thick french accent, only way to learn is from the textbook. Lecture slides are terrible
Mbb 201? More like French 201. Lectures only mandatory due to iclickers and quizzes.

Prof Northwood genuinely cares about her students. The course is pretty heavy, and she tries her best to teach you everything you need to know for the exams. If you want to do well in the course, study the Wiley questions, her lecture slides, and the tutorial questions.

dr northwood was an amazing prof!! she's so sweet and kind. all her exam questions were straight from Wiley so defs recommend purchasing it! she told what to study exactly on review days and told what her harder questions will ask. 10/10 would take another class w her

Dr. Northwood is one of the most passionate professors I have had. She obviously loves what she teaches, she is so sweet, and explains concepts very clearly relating it to real world situations. Her slides are way more detailed than her lectures so go to class to save the work.

Used the practice exams to prep for the real exams and got an A+ in the course.  Though, she said she'll make the course harder in the future.  Flips through a lot of slides, but tells you what's going to be on the test.  Was moody first few times I spoke to her, but when she saw I worked hard and was doing well, was much better after that.

Very knowledgeable professor and enthusiastic about the material. Told you what topics would be on exam at end of every powerpoint. Used textbook for background info and used some questions on exam. Funny in class - talked about Jersey Shore! Helpful when you asked questions. Great class overall, but she said in the future she would make it harder!

Overall super nice guy. His lectures can be a little confusing sometimes but honestly he's super caring and if you go to him in office hours, he's super helpful and doesn't make you feel bad. 10/10 would recommend him as a prof!!
He made the course very straightforward. He can be a bit boring and is usually hard to access via email but very knowledgeable and available after class for any help. Overall good and caring prof
Made everything clear that the professor didn't! One of the best TA's I've ever had in my years of university!

Where would I be without Onkar being there to make the concepts crystal clear?!  He is a lifesaver and should be the one teaching the course.  He is very approachable and is will to answer questions.  Thanks for your hard work (my friends and I really appreciate it)
